provisions.detail: Non-exclusive license to operate one Franchised Business. No right to open additional units or sublicense.
10-year initial term + 2 successor terms of 5 years each. Successor agreement may have materially different terms.
Must give 60-180 days notice; no defaults; sign then-current form; general release; $25K renewal fee; renovate to current standards; maintain lease through successor term.
5% royalty + 3% brand fund weekly; CPI adjustment clause (>5% threshold); EFT/ACH required
$100 per occurrence + 18%/year simple interest from original due date
Avg monthly (royalty+brand fund) × lesser of 36 or remaining months, min $30K. Covers only cash flow loss — franchisor may also pursue reputation/other damages separately.
Must use approved vendor for site selection. Franchisor must approve site. Must purchase/lease within 180 days of signing. Must open within 15 months.
Must use designated architect and project management firm. Franchisor does not assist with permits, building codes, or installation.
Must complete initial training to reasonable satisfaction. Failure to complete is non-curable default (Section 24.2.13).
Must comply with all specifications. Manual may be modified at franchisor's sole discretion. Modifications binding.
Bankruptcy filing, insolvency, receiver appointed, unsatisfied judgment >$10K for 60+ days.

17 enumerated triggers: failure to open, fraud/misrepresentation, law violation (10 days), criminal offense, under-reporting (2%+ twice in 24 months or 5%+ once), IP misuse, health/safety hazard, abandonment (3 days), failure to pay (10 days after demand), unauthorized transfer, brand covenant violation, license revocation, failure to complete training, repeated defaults (3+ in 12 months), cross-default, Franchise Owners Agreement breach, premises issues.
30-day cure for all other defaults. Franchisor may suspend its obligations during cure period.
Broadly defined: any direct/indirect, voluntary/involuntary assignment, sale, conveyance, including by merger, issuance of securities, operation of law, will, or trust.
30 days to match any offer; 30 additional days for due diligence; right restarts if terms change

12 conditions: 45-day notice, qualified transferee, terms not burdensome, all obligations paid, training completed, licenses obtained, new franchise agreement signed, $25K transfer fee, general release, broker fees, ongoing covenant compliance
No competing business, no >5% interest, no services for competitor — anywhere in the world
2 years; 10-mile radius from franchisee's location AND all other Boston's locations. Tolling for violations. Waiver of right to challenge as overly broad.
Franchisee waives right to challenge brand covenants as overly broad or unenforceable. (Voided in WA.)
All direct and indirect owners AND their spouses must guarantee all obligations. Unlimited personal guaranty.
Franchise managers and officers must sign noncompete/confidentiality
Mandatory non-binding mediation in Dallas, TX before arbitration. Parties share filing fee and mediator's fees.
Binding arbitration in Dallas, TX under AAA Commercial Rules. Single arbitrator. Texas law governs.
All disputes in Dallas, TX. Subject to state law overrides.

$5M GL, $1M workers comp, business interruption. A-VIII rated carrier. Franchisor as additional insured. May increase requirements at any time on 10 days notice. If franchisee fails to maintain, franchisor obtains at franchisee cost + 20% surcharge.
Broad indemnification of franchisor for any expenses/losses related to franchised business. Franchisor indemnifies franchisee only for IP claims if franchisee is in compliance.

Record all transactions per franchisor specs. Maintain books 7 years. Balance sheet/P&L/cash flow annually within 30 days of fiscal year end. Tax returns within 30 days of filing. $100/occurrence + $100/week late fee. Franchisor may use financial data in FDD and on intranet.
Fully assignable without prior notice. Franchisor remains liable for obligations only up to assignment date.
Only if franchisor is in material breach AND fails to cure within 60 days of written notice AND franchisee is in full compliance. Post-termination obligations still apply.

distinctive_clauses: Repeated defaults trigger: 3+ written notices of default in 12 months = non-curable default regardless of whether each was cured
Under-reporting trigger: 2%+ twice in 24 months or 5%+ once = non-curable
Abandonment trigger: only 3 business days without written authorization
Spousal guarantee required for ALL owners — unlimited personal liability
Waiver of right to challenge noncompete scope (voided in WA)
Franchisor may use franchisee financial data in FDD and on system intranet
Liquidated damages formula preserves separate claims for reputation and other damages
Franchisee termination requires 60-day cure (vs. franchisor's 30-day cure for franchisee defaults)
